title: Bank of England Agenda for Research, 2025-2028
summary: The Bank of England has published its second Agenda for Research (BEAR) covering 2025-2028, outlining key research priorities across five themes including the macroeconomic environment, financial system, prudential architecture, interconnected world, and central bank balance sheet. The document reflects on challenges including the period of high inflation and financial market turmoil since the first BEAR was published in 2020. The research agenda does not contain any immediate policy decisions or market-moving data. Note: PUBLISHED_AT date could not be verified.
key_points: The second BEAR introduces a new research theme on central bank balance sheets, addressing questions about balance sheet use for monetary policy and financial stability
Research priorities on inflation dynamics include studying how expectations evolve, non-linearities, asymmetries in high-inflation periods, and firms' price-setting behaviors
The Bernanke Review's recommendations call for enhanced forecasting frameworks and models to improve scenario analysis capabilities
Key transmission mechanism research areas include the role of non-bank financial institutions (NBFIs), heterogeneity across households and firms, and state-dependence of policy effects
Research will examine long-run drivers of UK productivity including technological change, intangible capital, and demography
The document mentions the Prudential Regulation Authority's new secondary competitiveness and growth objective introduced in 2023
why_it_matters: This research agenda signals the Bank of England's analytical priorities for the coming years and indicates where new understanding may inform future policy decisions. While not market-moving itself, it provides insight into the Bank's view that inflation dynamics, transmission mechanisms, and balance sheet policy require further research following the post-2020 inflation episode. External researchers are encouraged to collaborate on these themes.
macro_context: The agenda identifies changing inflation dynamics and transmission mechanisms as key research priorities, reflecting the Bank's view that economic relationships have changed in ways that warrant deeper understanding. Research will explore how monetary policy should respond to larger-than-normal shocks and series of shocks, and how production networks, wage-price interactions, and non-bank financial institutions affect the macroeconomy.
market_structure_context: The document notes financial market turmoil as a challenge since 2020 and identifies the central bank balance sheet as requiring new research to understand its role in monetary and financial stability policies.
